item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, the new picture is somehow even creepier than the old one. Bravo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For me, the creepiest part is this one:

E4…

kNIGHT night nite

KN0CK KN0CK WH0S THERE?

WE C Y0U, Agent Jack07

& THE PAWN & THE KING ARE IN THE SAME B0X

PAWN TAKES KIIIIIIIIIIIII11111111111111111111111111111111

+1

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The final image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Title: 1875-IMG-1AB-new.jpg
Author(s): Aussie world, JackalRelated
Release year: 2020
Note: Created by JackalRelated using the image below.

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Title: Wild Mouse at Aussie World.jpg
Author: Aussie world

The rest of the images have been removed due to non-compliance/consistency's sake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel that this page is unnecessarily long. It takes way to long to get to the point, and the individual pieces are so unrelated and weird. However, because of the cool username gimmick and the jump scare (i didn't even know you could do jump scares in text format), I gotta give it a no vote.

item.title: Re: The pawn and the king
item.content:encoded:
My guess? Given that it's followed by "Pawn takes King", I think it's not referring to the girls at all. In Chess, the King is essentially the player character, while the Pawn is the most nonthreatening, unassuming piece in the game. I think the King they're referring to isn't one of them, it's "you". You're the one in control here, and the automaton can't really hurt you if you don't let it. But they manipulate you into a position where they *can* hurt you, and then proceed to do so, by means of a suicidal nervous breakdown. As for them being in the same box? Maybe a rather contrived way of pointing out that they're still a threat to you, being in the same game and all.

Also, it used to be (and still is) considered bad form to actually *take* the King; the game is over when there's no way for the King to get out of being taken. These girls have no sense of fair play.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: continues to be one of the most overlooked articles on this website imo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't believe I just got jump scared by a wiki page. +1

item.title: The pawn and the king
item.content:encoded: Anyone else pick up on "the pawn and the king are in the same box"? I think this pretty clearly refers to the girls. However, what does this mean for how the view each other? To me it implies a somewhat if not outright antagonistic relationship given the difference in importance between the pawn and the king. This may also give a clue as to what's up with level 5.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: honestly this is great, I heard about it on a forum and I didn't understand how it could be "like not creepy, like actually made me drop my laptop" until I read the I C you [username] that actually made me jump a little. Take your +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted it because it's creative and pretty terrifying, but like- what the fuck does it do? After reading it 2+ times I still have not a clue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: sorry for so frequently commenting on really old entries, but I think that's the slowest I've ever scrolled down a page

item.title: Re: Creepy
item.content:encoded:
[[module ListUsers users="."]]

It's a funny idea, wasn't it, %%TITLE%%?

[[/module]]

… dammit

the day I figure what black magic they use to get it to work…

item.title: Re: Creepy
item.content:encoded: honestly, screw the username part, the image actually terrified me.

item.title: Re: Creepy
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this is my first time reading the article since I joined the wiki, and it really did give me a bit of a spook when I saw my username in there.

item.title: Creepy
item.content:encoded:
This is perhaps the only SCP to have scared (scared isn't really the best word for it, but it is the closest I could come to the alarm/worry I felt) while I was reading it, and not just later when it gets dark.

Also, did anyone else notice that the part where they say:

☐☐☐☐☒▦⚉☐☒☐☐☐☐☐☒▦⚉☐☒☐☐☐☐☐☒▦⚉☐

E4…

kNIGHT night nite

KN0CK KN0CK WH0S THERE?

WE C Y0U, >insert username<

& THE PAWN & THE KING ARE IN THE SAME B0X

PAWN TAKES KIIIIIIIIIIIII11111111111111111111111111111111

☐☐☐☐☒▦⚉☐☒☐☐☐☐☐☒▦⚉☐☒☐☐☐☐☐☒▦⚉☐

Only shows up if you are logged in? If you view it not logged in, that segment just isn't there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The most brilliant thing is that the author spend most of parts on writing how the chess machine works, and seems to mention the picture that could kills watcher inadvertently. It tells people you may die when you see the picture in subconsciousness without being taken seriously on it's only an article setup or not and scares us eventually.

+1, Sorry for my terrible English:(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Brilliant. One of the first skips that genuinely gave me the creeps.
+1

P.S I know this is late.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd seen the ending pic in several creepypasta forums before. Did it originate from this SCP? Or was the SCP adapted to match it?

In any case, the pic now gives me chills every time I scroll to it at the last addendum. It didn't use to give me chills before.

Masterful use of misdirection. Kudos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
scrolls down
Girls: O H H I
Masterfully executed, suitably creepy for the setting, I like the idea of a chess computer made of two girls and it makes some of the mistakes the machine makes far more interesting. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My favorite part of this article is how the fact that there's two daughters in the machine explains some of the weird shit. Like how on setting 5, the fastest setting, when it starts playing erratically, it could be because the two daughters are both trying to make different moves.

item.title: Excellent article
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed the pacing of this one. It builds the perfect amount of tension leading up to the "punchline", which was also really well-executed. I was left wanting to read more, which is always a good sign. Thanks!

item.title: Suggestion for improvement
item.content:encoded: ELO can't be computed from a single chess game. For each setting I through IV of an unknown rated chess engine (or human player) you have to run it through many games (same time controls for each) against opponents of known ratings in order to get a reliable estimate. The Foundation is going to want solid results so the more games err, experiments the better. At least 20 to 30, if not more, per "speed level" for reliability.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, the internet has raised the bar on jump scares .

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Someone else in the thread said that they think this image is an edited version of two kids riding a rollercoaster or something along those lines - basically the colorized version would show that their faces are just really blurry and that they're having a generally good time.

I kind of like the concept of them just realizing they're spooky ghosts and having fun terrorizing the Foundation (what else are you going to do if you've been stuck in a chess playing machine for over a century?) as opposed to them being tormented spirits of the damned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's been a really, really long time since an image made me vocalize anything resembling fear. Good document, good ending, +1 for making an old man jump.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should I feel bad for these girls? looks like they're in vain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to the adequate buildup of tension and explanation of the twin sisters being turned to a chess automaton, this article does not feel (too much) like simply an excuse to post a jump scare pic.

So, +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tnis is my heart breaker

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the heart attack.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was sincerely terrifying. Absolutely well done. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Big fan, even when I expected the photo at the end, I still got startled by it. Keep up the good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know if it's quite too late to be asking this, but where did you get that last picture from? Did you happen to edit a pre-existing one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god. I never thought it was possible to put a jump scare in static text.
I had found this one via one of those memes labeling different stills from spongebob episodes with names of things (in this case SCPs, for example -079 as Karen and whoops I’m on a tangent). Seeing that there was a chess SCP, I got excited because I had gotten into it a while back. I was hoping for a light-hearted one and was hoping for the best when I read the part about the human-powered chess “machine” (which I recognized, props for that) but got tipped off by the whole human body parts thing… especially those of a chess champion’s daughter, wow… extra points for implied being droven mad by your own game! The text jumpscare made me a bit angry at myself, but I appreciate the many lines of text before it so that I knew what was coming and scrolled down fast enough to only see half of the picture. Oh, forgot to mention I’ve also been teaching myself Cyrillic lately and I liked the little “checkmate” you slipped in there.
Still on the fence for my vote, but the only thing in my way are the spoopy twins, so half vote I guess?

TL;DR cool chess and Russian language references and good creep factor until I got jump scared and butthurt, putting me in a state of denial with giving an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This spooked me, so it deserves an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
what a sore loser.
i really dig the whole meta thing. i haven't seen it done that well before, it seems really hard to execute properly, but it's hella cool here. i was honestly surprised to see my username mixed in there!
my only question is did anyone ever try to have an email conversation with this friendo? they seem pretty sad. maybe they just want a friend? im pretty sure you could hook up a system that doesn't download files like images or spooky murder .exes.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
Hey guys, this scared the spoop out of me while I read it in broad daylight. It was requested of me, and I put it together the best I could to keep the creepy spirit. I hope you guys like it! Excellent read, author!

https://youtu.be/5Tur3mScy8Y

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was at work while reading this, surrounded by people and I recoiled. You rascal, you. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Addendum-03 Really got me good. Especially because my name literally rhymes with the statement so it worked very well. Oh yeah and everything else was good too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why did I choose to read this at midnight? Oh yeah…

Have all the upvotes I can now muster from my sleepless soul.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad I wasn't planning on sleeping tonight, because that picture…brrrr. I probably should have expected it after the corrupted parts, but my curiosity got the best of me.

Honestly, I think the lots of stuff that seems kind of irrelevant works, because it puts the reader off guard. Not that I particularly LIKE being jump-scared, but the point of a jump-scare is not being ready for it, and it did its job. Besides, it's an interesting object even before that.

Thanks for all the upvote love.

I understand that I owe many of you new pairs of underwear. Just know that the underwear I send you may be haunted, cursed, &/or made of spiders.

Also, I finally made an author page:

Dr. Incandenza's Personnel File

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My username is Ogilvy…

I choose this as a made up name from 1984 but I think it is used as a pawn for selfish higher ups [/spoiler

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel as though this article is underrated.
It is one of the only SCPs that actually scared me. And i dont think its just the picture. SCP 106 has a creepy picture, as does SCP 122. But they just dont scare me like this one does.
You scared me, and thats hard to do.
So for that, i give you my upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it's good because it's not just a jump scare. It's like a magic trick where the magician explains ahead of time exactly how they're going to fool you, and then they fool you anyway.

It describes the scary image, so that's not really a surprise. It states that it anomalously affects computers, so that's not really a surprise. And yet, when it had my name in the log the hair on my neck stood up, and when I saw the image it freaked me out and I was genuinely unsure whether it was moving or not. Telling before showing enhanced the effect by priming me for it.

The "unnecessary" material, to me, is the magician's patter, the distracting hand gestures that allow the trick to occur right in front of the subject without them noticing.

+1 for all the right reasons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know. It feels a little bit too much like, "BOO HAHA TRICKED YOU." Now, granted, I didn't get much sleep last night because of it, but that's just because the image used was terrifying. I'm just not a huge fan of the whole concept; it feels like two or three objects that would make good SCPs on their own (haunted chess machine, haunted samurai armor, girls' ghosts haunting e-mail, etc.), but that, when mashed together, really don't work as a whole.
I completely understand why other people like it, it's just really not my cup of tea.

item.title: Just.... what?
item.content:encoded:
I know, we all love it for the jumpscare and everything, but it still seems like there are some loose ends here that need to be answered.

The addendum says "erratic piece movement was observed. The speed at which SCP-1875 moved its pieces increased, and it began to violently ram pieces together on the board" and "During game ██, illegal moves were observed, forcing us to abandon games-in-progress. This activity was seen more frequently as testing progressed." Why? We're given no reason for it and it doesn't seem to have anything to do with the emails. Is it angry? Is it sentient and the girls/mind are getting frustrated? We're weren't told weather it was losing. Why does it send out the emails? What does "a1 b2 c3 d4 e5 f6 h7 g8" mean? And "THE PAWN & THE KING ARE IN THE SAME B0X"? I… What?

There's just too much going on here for me to give it an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This remains the only article on this site with a successful jumpscare.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm doing a sequential reread of all the SCPs and I've read this for the first time back when the footnote spoiled the final image and nearly pooped myself, and I was so absurdly reluctant to see it now just in the mere memory of that experience. Legendary.

Take my drop-in-the-bucket three-year-belated not-a-lurker-anymore upvote, you deserve it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Normally I really hate jumpscares, but damn, this feels well executed, have an upvote :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought that it started performing poorly at step 5 because that transferred control to the girls.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like how the reported symptoms are spaced so that you won't feel them all right away, so after viewing the image there's that niggling voice in the back of your head saying "yup, your fucked now."

+1

item.title: Re: wtf?
item.content:encoded: Yes, it's a code block that inserts the viewer's username if it's logged in.

item.title: wtf?
item.content:encoded: ok, is my name supposed to be there? well, not my name, but what i want to change it to, because it is an obvious alias

item.title: The Picture...
item.content:encoded: Throughout this I was like "ok…not too creepy…" But that picture…I scrolled past it really fast and I didn't even see it that well but I almost dropped my phone when I saw it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The last 2 collapsibles (and the creepy use of the readers name) are so well placed, i cought myself getting nervous while scrolling down to the photograph.

Very well made, deserves the upvote!

But… hold on a second… i want to see the photo again…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goddamn it and I was reading this at 3AM

item.title: Re: What on Earth...?
item.content:encoded: You should read up on the Mechanical Turk hoax. That's what this is based on. The armor makes sense in context.

item.title: Re: What on Earth...?
item.content:encoded: It's a red herring to throw you off track for the jumpscare at the end. The more false leads, the more likely you'll put your guard down, when in reality, the armor adds nothing of importance to the skip.

item.title: Re: What on Earth...?
item.content:encoded:
Presents a solid image. A chess-playing set of empty samurai armor is a more striking image than just a chess board that plays itself. IRL, I'd be more unsettled by the armor than by a chess set that just does its own thing.

Also, sometimes, authors here let Rule of Cool get in the way.

item.title: What on Earth...?
item.content:encoded:
Okay. The one thing about this SCP article that perplexes me the most is not the picture attached to Addendum-04, or the fact that the page refers to the reader by their username, but the GUSOKU STYLE SAMURAI ARMOR.

Someone PLEASE explain to me why the heck it's there aside from the whole "performance prop" thing! Seriously. I have no idea why, in the unholy name of Yomi, that a chess machine running off of the souls of twin daughters of a Russian chess player would have a set of armor that comes from Japan!
